text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH CONTRAST
INTERIM RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION: 58-year-old female with known high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, FIGO stage IIIC. Status post primary debulking surgery including TAH-BSO, omentectomy, pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection, rectosigmoid resection, and splenectomy. Now on adjuvant carboplatin/paclitaxel chemotherapy; this examination obtained after completion of cycle 3 for interim restaging.

COMPARISON: Prior staging CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis (pre-operative).

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of intravenous iodinated contrast (100 mL Omnipaque 350) in the portal venous phase. Oral contrast was also administered. Coronal and sagittal reformats were reviewed.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:
Lungs: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules identified. No consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pleural-based masses. The previously noted small bilateral pleural effusions have resolved. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.
Mediastinum and hila: Heart size is normal. No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. Thyroid not fully imaged.
Chest wall and bones: No suspicious osseous lesion identified in the imaged thorax.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and attenuation. No focal hepatic lesion identified. The previously documented periportal lymphadenopathy has resolved. No biliary ductal dilation. Post-surgical changes are noted in the right upper quadrant consistent with prior cholecystectomy — surgical clips are seen in the gallbladder fossa; no gallbladder is identified, as expected.
Spleen: The spleen is absent consistent with prior splenectomy. The splenectomy bed demonstrates expected postoperative fibrofatty changes without discrete fluid collection or mass.
Pancreas: The pancreas appears unremarkable. No ductal dilation. No peripancreatic fat stranding.
Kidneys and adrenal glands: The kidneys are symmetric in size and show normal enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses. The adrenal glands are unremarkable.
Stomach and bowel: Postsurgical changes are present in keeping with prior rectosigmoid resection; surgical staple line is identified in the pelvis with no anastomotic leak or peri-anastomotic fluid collection evident. The remainder of the visualized small and large bowel appears within normal limits. No bowel obstruction or wall thickening.
Peritoneum and omentum: The omentum is absent consistent with prior omentectomy. No peritoneal soft tissue nodularity or enhancement is identified. No ascites. The mesenteric fat planes are clean without discrete nodal masses or implants. No serosal deposits seen along the right or left hemidiaphragm, paracolic gutters, or bowel surfaces.
Retroperitoneum: No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The aorta and IVC are normal. No retroperitoneal mass or fluid collection.

PELVIS:
Uterus and adnexa: The uterus and bilateral adnexa are absent consistent with prior total hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. The pelvic surgical bed demonstrates expected postoperative changes including a small amount of pelvic fibrofatty soft tissue and surgical clips, without evidence of local recurrence or discrete mass.
Bladder: The urinary bladder appears unremarkable. No wall thickening or intraluminal filling defect.
Pelvic lymph nodes: No pelvic sidewall or inguinal lymphadenopathy. The external and internal iliac nodal chains are unremarkable.
Pelvic floor: No pelvic free fluid. No pelvic mass.

BONES: No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesion identified throughout the imaged skeleton.

SOFT TISSUES: No subcutaneous or soft tissue masses. Small subcutaneous seroma/fluid collection noted along the lower midline abdominal wall incision site, measuring approximately 2.3 cm in greatest dimension — this is felt to represent a postoperative seroma and appears stable in appearance; no surrounding cellulitis or drainable fluid collection.

IMPRESSION:
1. No evidence of residual or recurrent peritoneal carcinomatosis. Resolution of the previously documented omental cake, peritoneal implants, and ascites.
2. No new hepatic, pulmonary, or distant metastatic disease identified.
3. Lymph nodes are within normal limits throughout the chest, abdomen, and pelvis.
4. Expected postoperative changes related to prior debulking surgery, including absence of spleen, gallbladder, uterus, and bilateral adnexa; rectosigmoid anastomosis appears intact.
5. Small lower midline abdominal wall seroma, likely related to prior surgery — clinically correlate.
6. Overall, a markedly improved examination compared to the pre-operative staging study, with no detectable residual disease.
